pattern: The degree is the peak frequency; the shortest qualifying window is the span between the first and last occurrence of some element reaching that frequency.
hints: First figure out the degree itself: the largest number of times any single value repeats.
For each value that ties for the maximum frequency, the smallest window containing all of its copies runs from its first index to its last index.
Track first index, last index, and count per value in one pass, then minimise (last - first + 1) over the values whose count equals the degree.

edge_inputs.description: single element has degree one and window one
all identical, whole array is the window
two values tie for degree, choose the tighter span
the dominant value is spread to the ends
all distinct, every degree is one, answer is one
winner is a tight cluster at the front beating a spread tie
later tie has a shorter span than the earlier one

stress.description: many repeats over a small value domain so several values reach high frequency
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: small domain forces ties at the degree

stress.description: wide value domain with sparse repeats
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 49999) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
